skill.name: resmi-yazi
skill.description: Türkçe resmi yazı, dilekçe, itiraz mektubu ve kurumsal yazışma üretimi. Devlet kurumlarına dilekçe, SGK/vergi idaresi yazışması, Noter belgesi, şikâyet mektubu, iş başvurusu için kullan.
skill.summary: Generate official Turkish letters, petitions, appeals, and corporate correspondence for government institutions, tax authorities, and job applications.
skill.author: cesareth

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 16 potential issues (backtick patterns and weak crypto references), but all are false positives. The backticks are Markdown code block delimiters for Turkish letter templates, not shell execution. The 'weak crypto' detections are Turkish words containing 'md' (e.g., 'MÜDÜRLÜĞÜ'NE' = 'TO THE DIRECTORATE'). This is a content-only skill with no executable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Blocks
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected Ruby/shell backtick patterns at lines 37-213, but these are Markdown code block delimiters (```) containing Turkish letter templates. No executable code present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Turkish Language Detection
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 'md' patterns as weak cryptographic algorithms, but these are Turkish words: 'üretimi' (production), 'MÜDÜRLÜĞÜ'NE' (to the directorate). No actual cryptography or hashing algorithms used.

content.user_title: Generate Official Turkish Letters and Petitions
content.value_statement: Create properly formatted official correspondence for Turkish government institutions, tax authorities, and corporate entities. Save time with culturally accurate templates that follow Turkish administrative writing standards.
actual_capabilities: Generate properly formatted Turkish petition letters (dilekçe) following official structure
Create correspondence for Turkish government institutions including SGK and tax authorities
Provide employment-related letters such as resignation notices and job applications
Generate legal notices including tenant eviction warnings (ihtarname)
Follow Turkish administrative writing conventions (formal 'siz' address, passive voice, proper citations)
limitations: Templates follow Turkish administrative standards and may not apply to other jurisdictions
Does not provide legal advice - users should consult attorneys for legal matters
Requires manual input of personal details (TC kimlik number, addresses, dates)
Generated letters may need notarization for official legal validity

use_cases.title: Government Petition Submission
use_cases.description: Citizens submitting formal requests to Turkish government agencies need properly formatted dilekçe that follow administrative writing conventions.
use_cases.target_user: Turkish citizens interacting with government institutions

use_cases.title: Employment Documentation
use_cases.description: Employees and job seekers create formal resignation letters, job applications, and employment-related correspondence in Turkish.

use_cases.title: Legal Correspondence
use_cases.description: Landlords, tenants, and individuals generate formal notices and legal letters requiring proper Turkish legal formatting.
use_cases.target_user: Individuals handling legal or property matters in Turkey

prompt_templates.title: Basic Petition Generator
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Turkish petition letter to [INSTITUTION] regarding [TOPIC]. Include my details: [NAME], [TC NUMBER], [ADDRESS]. I am requesting [SPECIFIC REQUEST].
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate a basic formal petition for government institutions

prompt_templates.title: SGK Service Document Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an SGK Hizmet Belgesi talep dilekçesi for [NAME] with TC number [TCNO], SGK sicil number [SGCNO], employed at [COMPANY] from [START DATE] to [END DATE].
prompt_templates.scenario: Request social security service records from Turkish Social Security Institution

prompt_templates.title: Resignation Letter
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a formal istifa (resignation) letter to [COMPANY] HR department for employee [NAME] in role [POSITION] since [START DATE], resigning effective [RESIGNATION DATE] with [NOTICE PERIOD] notice period.
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a professional resignation notice following Turkish labor law conventions

prompt_templates.title: Tax Appeal Letter
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a Vergi İncelemesi Kararına İtiraz letter to [TAX OFFICE] for taxpayer [NAME] with tax ID [VKN/TC], appealing decision dated [DATE] with reference number [REF NO], citing grounds: [APPEAL REASONS].
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate formal appeal letter challenging tax assessment decisions

output_examples.input: Write a petition to the Istanbul Metropolitan Municipality requesting tree pruning in my neighborhood. My name is Ahmet Yılmaz, TC: 12345678901, living in Kadıköy.
output_examples.output: A complete formal petition letter addressed to İstanbul Büyükşehir Belediyesi Başkanı, with proper header including date and city, subject line requesting 'Mahallede Ağaç Budama Talebi', formal introduction of the petitioner, detailed explanation of the request citing neighborhood safety concerns, proper legal closure with 'saygılarımla arz ederim', and signature block including name, TC number, address, and contact information.

output_examples.input: Create a job application letter for a marketing manager position at TechTurkiye Inc.
output_examples.output: A professional application letter in Turkish addressing the İnsan Kaynakları Departmanı, with position-specific subject line, formal introduction expressing interest in the Marketing Manager role, concise summary of relevant marketing experience and qualifications, specific mention of why the applicant wants to work at TechTurkiye Inc., highlight of 2-3 key competencies with concrete achievements, respectful closing requesting interview consideration, and complete contact information including phone, email, and LinkedIn.

best_practices: Always warn users about including sensitive personal information like TC kimlik numbers in generated documents
Remind users that legal documents should be reviewed by qualified attorneys before submission
Recommend users keep copies of all official correspondence for their records
Suggest notarization for documents requiring legal validity (ihtarname, official contracts)
anti_patterns: Do not generate documents containing real personal information - use placeholders like [AD SOYAD] and [TC KİMLİK NO]
Do not provide legal advice or guarantee outcomes of legal proceedings
Do not modify the formal Turkish administrative style (e.g., using 'ben' instead of 'tarafımca')
Do not create documents for fraudulent purposes or misrepresentation of facts

faq.question: What types of official letters can this skill generate?
faq.answer: This skill generates Turkish petitions (dilekçe), government correspondence, employment letters, legal notices, and formal business communications following Turkish administrative writing standards.

faq.question: Are the generated letters legally valid?
faq.answer: The templates follow proper formatting but are not legal documents. For legal matters, consult an attorney. Some documents like ihtarname may require notarization for legal validity.

faq.question: Should I include my real TC kimlik number when generating letters?
faq.answer: The skill uses placeholders like [TC KIMLIK NO] for safety. Only include your real Turkish ID number in the final document when you are ready to submit it.

faq.question: Can I use these templates for English-language correspondence?
faq.answer: No, these templates are specifically designed for Turkish-language official correspondence and follow Turkish administrative conventions. Use different templates for English documents.

faq.question: What is the difference between dilekçe and ihtarname?
faq.answer: A dilekçe is a general petition or request to government institutions. An ihtarname is a formal legal notice used to warn or notify someone of legal consequences, often requiring notarization.

faq.question: Do I need a lawyer to use these templates?
faq.answer: For routine administrative matters like document requests or job applications, you may not need a lawyer. For legal disputes, tax appeals, or eviction notices, consult a Turkish attorney.
